verses.1: It was now just before the Passover Feast, and Jesus knew that His hour had come to leave this world and return to the Father. Having loved His own who were in the world, He loved them to the very end.
verses.2: The evening meal was underway, and the devil had already put into the heart of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, to betray Jesus.
verses.3: Jesus knew that the Father had delivered all things into His hands, and that He had come from God and was returning to God.
verses.4: So He got up from the supper, laid aside His outer garments, and wrapped a towel around His waist.
verses.5: After that, He poured water into a basin and began to wash the disciples' feet and dry them with the towel that was around Him.
verses.6: He came to Simon Peter, who asked Him, "Lord, are You going to wash my feet?"

segments.text: Jesus replied,
"You do not realize now what I am doing, but later you will understand."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "Never shall You wash my feet!" Peter told Him. Jesus answered,
"Unless I wash you, you have no part with Me."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.9: "Then, Lord," Simon Peter replied, "not only my feet, but my hands and my head as well!"

segments.text: Jesus told him,
"Whoever has already bathed needs only to wash his feet, and he will be completely clean. And you are clean, though not all of you."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For He knew who would betray Him. That is why He said,
"Not all of you are clean."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When Jesus had washed their feet and put on His outer garments, He reclined with them again and asked,
"Do you know what I have done for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

13.text: You call Me Teacher and Lord, and rightly so, because I am.
13.speaker: Jesus

14.text: So if I, your Lord and Teacher, have washed your feet, you also should wash one another's feet.
14.speaker: Jesus

15.text: I have set you an example so that you should do as I have done for you.
15.speaker: Jesus

16.text: Truly, truly, I tell you, no servant is greater than his master, nor is a messenger greater than the one who sent him.
16.speaker: Jesus

17.text: If you know these things, you will be blessed if you do them.
17.speaker: Jesus

18.text: I am not speaking about all of you; I know whom I have chosen. But this is to fulfill the Scripture: 'The one who shares My bread has lifted up his heel against Me.'
18.speaker: Jesus

19.text: I am telling you now before it happens, so that when it comes to pass, you will believe that I am He.
19.speaker: Jesus

20.text: Truly, truly, I tell you, whoever receives the one I send receives Me, and whoever receives Me receives the One who sent Me."
20.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After Jesus had said this, He became troubled in spirit and testified,
"Truly, truly, I tell you, one of you will betray Me."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.22: The disciples looked at one another, perplexed as to which of them He meant.
verses.23: One of His disciples, the one whom Jesus loved, was reclining at His side.
verses.24: So Simon Peter motioned to him to ask Jesus which one He was talking about.
verses.25: Leaning back against Jesus, he asked, "Lord, who is it?"

segments.text: Jesus answered,
"It is the one to whom I give this morsel after I have dipped it."
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then He dipped the morsel and gave it to Judas son of Simon Iscariot.
And when Judas had taken the morsel, Satan entered into him. Then Jesus said to Judas,
"What you are about to do, do quickly."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.28: But no one at the table knew why Jesus had said this to him.
verses.29: Since Judas kept the money bag, some thought that Jesus was telling him to buy what was needed for the feast, or to give something to the poor.
verses.30: As soon as he had received the morsel, Judas went out into the night.

segments.text: When Judas had gone out, Jesus said,
"Now the Son of Man is glorified, and God is glorified in Him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

32.text: If God is glorified in Him, God will also glorify the Son in Himself, and He will glorify Him at once.
32.speaker: Jesus

33.text: Little children, I am with you only a little while longer. You will look for Me, and as I said to the Jews, so now I say to you: 'Where I am going, you cannot come.'
33.speaker: Jesus

34.text: A new commandment I give you: Love one another. As I have loved you, so you also must love one another.
34.speaker: Jesus

35.text: By this everyone will know that you are My disciples, if you love one another."
35.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: "Lord, where are You going?" Simon Peter asked. Jesus answered,
"Where I am going, you cannot follow Me now, but you will follow later."
segments.speaker: Jesus

verses.37: "Lord," said Peter, "why can't I follow You now? I will lay down my life for You."

segments.text: "Will you lay down your life for Me?" Jesus replied.
"Truly, truly, I tell you, before the rooster crows, you will deny Me three times."
segments.speaker: Jesus

metadata.source: Derived from the Berean Standard Bible (BSB). Missing verses restored from the Textus Receptus tradition for completeness.
